summary: McKenzie notes that his education-focused software business (Bingo Card Creator) experiences severe seasonality, with sales dropping to near zero during summer when schools are out of session. He has gone roughly a week without a sale and expects the drought to last until mid-August when the new school term begins. He plans to use the downtime productively by improving his website and marketing. He also mentions needing to find a job by late July.
key_points: Education market software has extreme seasonality — sales virtually stop when school is not in session
The summer sales drought is predictable and lasts roughly three months
Downtime can be used productively for website improvements and marketing work
At this stage McKenzie still needed a day job alongside his software business
money_quotes: One of the unfortunate facts of the education market is that it has pretty severe seasonality: sales drop to close to nothing when school is not in session.
I have had roughly a week without a sale now, and expect that sales will remain severely depressed until roughly August 15th.
Start of the new term means cash money.
Oh well, more time to improve the website, marketing, and there is that minor issue of finding myself a job by July 25th…
key_insight: Education-market software businesses face severe seasonal revenue drops during summer, making it essential to use downtime for improving the product and marketing ahead of the next buying cycle.
